title: Taxidermy Antelope Mount and Warm Timber Paneling at Llao Llao Hotel in Bariloche Rio Negro
description: San Carlos de Bariloche, Río Negro: A classic taxidermy antelope trophy rests against the rich, honey-toned cedar paneling that defines the interior architecture of the historic Llao Llao Hotel. The subject is captured in a crisp profile shot, highlighting the deep, dark, and spiraled texture of the horns against the soft, vertical grain of the surrounding woodwork. The lighting accentuates the play between the natural wood textures and the smooth, preserved hide of the mount, creating a sense of refined, rustic atmosphere typical of Patagonian mountain lodges. Every detail, from the sharp focus on the glass eye to the intricate ridges of the antlers, suggests a meticulous preservation effort within the hotel’s storied walls. Such decor choices reflect the traditional Andean hunting heritage and the European-influenced architectural aesthetic that has become synonymous with luxury travel in this region. The interplay of warm light and timber surfaces invites viewers to appreciate the intersection of natural history and high-end hospitality in southern Argentina. This composition underscores the hotel's commitment to maintaining a timeless, authentic mountain-retreat character that blends seamlessly with the surrounding landscape of the Nahuel Huapi National Park. By documenting these subtle interior details, the frame preserves a fragment of the hotel's enduring legacy as a premier destination for travelers seeking both comfort and cultural immersion in the Andes. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
